market_data_input.key_trends: Secteur leader: energy (+37.6% YTD) | Région leader: south-korea (+31.4% YTD) | 5 secteur(s) en refroidissement (3M ≤ -2.0%) | 52W leader: energy (+32.5% sur 12 mois) | 6M leader: energy (+33.8% sur 6 mois)
market_data_input.risks: Secteurs en difficulté: financials, communication-services, information-technology | Régions en difficulté: germany, switzerland, italy | Concentration: peu de secteurs en zone favorable (rotation possible) | 5 secteurs avec 6M négatif (momentum moyen-terme dégradé)
market_data_input.sector_momentum_summary: materials(neutral,b=0.85,d=0.03%), energy(favored,b=0.74,d=1.76%), financials(avoided,b=0.94,d=-1.63%), communication-services(avoided,b=1.12,d=-2.51%), information-technology(avoided,b=1.33,d=-1.22%), consumer-staples(neutral,b=0.25,d=1.34%), industrials(neutral,b=0.9,d=-0.61%), utilities(neutral,b=0.37,d=-0.81%), real-estate(neutral,b=0.52,d=-0.22%), healthcare(avoided,b=0.55,d=-1.19%), consumer-discretionary(avoided,b=1.1,d=-2.14%)

ai_response.regime_rationale: Brent near $100 + PCE at 2.8% (above target) + Fed on hold for 6 months + VIX 22 + tech/financials/consumer-discretionary all declining while energy leads massively (+37.6% YTD) = stagflationary dynamics. Growth signals mixed (S&P up 4% today suggests relief rally, but sector rotation into defensives/energy and away from growth/cyclicals is classic late-cycle stagflation pattern).

adjustments.action: Increase energy/oil cap — dominant sector, stagflation beneficiary
adjustments.rationale: Brent $98 + energy +37.6% YTD + only favored sector in RADAR; oil supply tightness feeds through to earnings and acts as inflation hedge.

adjustments.action: Reduce AI/tech cap — high beta, rate-sensitive, momentum negative
adjustments.rationale: IT avoided by RADAR, Nasdaq underperforming S&P today (1% vs 4.1%), high duration equity suffers when real rates stay elevated and PCE sticky at 2.8%.

adjustments.action: Reduce semi cap — cyclical + avoided by momentum
adjustments.rationale: Semiconductors are high-beta cyclicals in an environment where growth is decelerating and IT sector momentum is negative on 3M and 6M timeframes.

adjustments.action: Increase gold hedge — inflation protection + geopolitical premium
adjustments.rationale: Gold at $4784 despite 10% drawdown from ATH signals massive structural bid; stagflation + dollar weakening (DTWEXBGS 118.9 = neutral-to-weak) + real rates plateau = gold supportive.

adjustments.action: Increase precious metals cap — silver momentum + inflation hedge
adjustments.rationale: Silver at $78 confirms broad precious metals bid; industrial + monetary demand in stagflation historically outperforms.

adjustments.action: Prefer TIPS, shorten duration, avoid HY
adjustments.rationale: PCE 2.8% + 5Y breakeven 2.61% + Brent $98 = inflation persistence; TIPS protect real purchasing power while short duration limits rate risk with Fed on hold and 10Y at 4.31%.

adjustments.action: Raise bond floor for Stable — increase defensive allocation
adjustments.rationale: Stable profile at 43.7% bonds should increase given stagflationary uncertainty; short-duration TIPS and T-bills yield 4%+ with minimal risk.

adjustments.action: Increase dividend/value cap — defensive quality in stagflation
adjustments.rationale: Consumer staples +1.34% today outperforming broad market on risk-adjusted basis; dividend/value provides income + lower beta in stagflationary regime.

bond_strategy.regime_summary: Short-duration TIPS + T-bills core; avoid HY (spreads at 294bps look tight for stagflation risk, asymmetric downside); IG acceptable only in short maturities.

cash_allocation.rationale: VIX 22 + today's 4% S&P spike smells like short-squeeze/relief rally rather than regime change; keep dry powder to buy dips if energy corrects or if stagflation deepens into risk-off. Stable lower because T-bills (CLTL, GBIL) already serve as productive cash.

crypto_allocation.rationale: Crypto is a risk asset that correlates with tech/liquidity; stagflation + Fed on hold = limited liquidity expansion. Reduce caps but don't eliminate for Agressif given gold/BTC correlation thesis.

warnings: TODAY'S +4.1% S&P MOVE: Likely short-squeeze or tariff/policy relief headline — NOT a regime change signal. Do not chase. Wait for confirmation over 3-5 sessions.
HY SPREADS AT 294bps LOOK COMPLACENT: In stagflation, HY defaults rise with a lag. Current spreads price in soft landing, not stagflation. Asymmetric risk to the downside.
BRENT $98 → $100+ TRIGGER: Break above $100 would accelerate stagflation narrative, potentially forcing Fed into impossible choice (hike into weakness). Monitor weekly.
DOLLAR WEAKENING (DTWEXBGS 118.9, EUR/USD 1.178): If dollar continues weakening, imported inflation rises further, reinforcing stagflation. But weak dollar helps EM and commodities — mixed signal.
SECTOR CONCENTRATION RISK: Only energy in 'favored' zone. If oil corrects (demand destruction, SPR release, OPEC+ surprise), the only working trade unwinds violently.
PCE 2.8% vs CPI 2.4% DIVERGENCE: Fed watches PCE — the gap suggests services inflation stickier than headline suggests. Risk of hawkish surprise if PCE doesn't converge down.

market_interpretation.geopolitical_risk: Brent near $100 implies elevated geopolitical/supply risk (Middle East tensions, OPEC+ discipline, or sanctions); energy sector dominance confirms market pricing in sustained supply tightness.

adjustments_output.cash_tactical_rationale: VIX 22 + today's 4% S&P spike smells like short-squeeze/relief rally rather than regime change; keep dry powder to buy dips if energy corrects or if stagflation deepens into risk-off. Stable lower because T-bills (CLTL, GBIL) already serve as productive cash.
